close_reason: Replaced .to_vec() with direct [u8;32] conversion from GenericArray
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:226 computes 'Blob::new(digest.to_vec())'. Sha256::digest returns GenericArray<u8, U32>; aws_smithy_types::Blob::new takes Into<Vec<u8>>; GenericArray<u8, U32> already implements Into<Vec<u8>>. The .to_vec() is therefore a redundant 32-byte allocation on every signing call.

Fix: 'Blob::new(digest)' (or 'Blob::new(digest.as_slice().to_vec())' if a future generic-array version breaks the impl, but currently unnecessary).

Impact at scale: one extra 32-byte heap allocation per sign(). Trivial in absolute terms but it is on the documented hot path of an HSM signing backend, and the cleanup is one-line. Worth fixing, but not P0/P1.
title: Sha256::digest result is to_vec()'d unnecessarily
